selected_backend.configuration: one transaction-scoped database handle per repository-lock transaction with durable commits

rejected_production_backend.reason: Correctness tied, but the write-gate critical path and distribution surface favor redb. SQLite remains probe-only evidence and is not a runtime fallback.

rationale: Both candidates passed every frozen correctness oracle, so performance and delivery surfaces may be ranked.
Durable mutation is the write-gate critical path; redb won its p50 comparison in every measured target and mode.
redb preserves the one-native-Rust-engine delivery shape with smaller binaries, fewer transitive packages, and no bundled C source.
SQLite's faster Linux bounded queries, lower peak RSS, and smaller store remain explicit tradeoffs rather than being hidden.
Production ships only redb 4.1.0. There is no dual backend, migration target choice, or runtime fallback.
remaining_phase0_gates: Windows/Linux package, native path/root, and packaged skill evidence
public process-reopen, operation-recovery, pagination, cold/warm, and jobs determinism evidence
approved numeric Phase 1 time, RSS, and binary-size budgets
exact Git binding and independent review of the backend-selection architecture amendment
